title: Glandularia pumila
intro: Glandularia pumila, also known as Dwarf Verbena or Small Verbena, is a low-growing, perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Glandularia pumila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Glandularia pumila include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Glandularia pumila is classified in the family Verbenaceae, which includes a group of flowering plants commonly known as the vervain or verbena family. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species. Glandularia pumila is a member of the genus Glandularia, which includes several species of flowering plants native to the Americas. The plant's classification also reflects its unique features, such as its small size and delicate flowers. Within the Verbenaceae family, Glandularia pumila is one of many species that share similar characteristics and traits.
uses: Glandularia pumila is reported to have medicinal properties, primarily due to its antioxidants and phenolic compounds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and gastrointestinal problems. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and prevent infections. Additionally, the plant is believed to possess antidiabetic properties, helping to regulate blood sugar levels. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Glandularia pumila as a medicinal plant.

side_effects: Glandularia pumila, a plant commonly known as dwarf verbena, can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals due to its chemical composition.
